target.title: github activity workflow can drop issue/PR events before ClawSweeper dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

The `github activity to openclaw` workflow uses a repo-wide concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: true`. During bursts of GitHub activity, new activity runs cancel older activity runs before they necessarily feed/dispatch ClawSweeper. That can leave newly opened or updated issues/PRs with no ClawSweeper comment and no labels.

This is separate from #304, which covers schema-invalid `decision.rootCauseCluster` output after Codex has already run. This issue covers activity events that can be lost before a ClawSweeper review run is reliably created.

## Evidence

Current main has this workflow-level concurrency in `.github/workflows/github-activity.yml`:

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: >-
    github-activity-${{ github.event.client_payload.activity.repo || github.event.repository.full_name || github.repository }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_run' && 'workflow-run' || 'activity' }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

For normal issue/PR/comment activity this groups all activity for a target repository into the same `...-activity` group. It does not include the item number, event id, action, or delivery identity.

Live behavior observed on June 17, 2026:

- In a 200-run sample of `github activity to openclaw`, 176 runs were cancelled, 22 were skipped, 1 succeeded, and 1 was queued.
- A later 20-run sample showed the same pattern: mostly short cancelled `github_activity` runs, with only the newest queued/pending/in-progress runs surviving.

## Impact

If a run is cancelled before `Feed activity to OpenClaw` completes, ClawSweeper may never receive the event. From the target repository this presents as issues/PRs that have neither a ClawSweeper review comment nor ClawSweeper-applied labels.

This likely explains the no-comment/no-label bucket that is not addressed by #304 or by exact-review capacity timeout recovery.

## Expected behavior

Issue/PR activity should be captured durably before cancellation can lose it. Possible fix directions:

- include item/event identity in the concurrency group for item activity;
- avoid `cancel-in-progress: true` for issue/PR opened/reopened/synchronize activity;
- write a durable event ledger before any cancellable work;
- add a scheduled reconciliation/backfill that finds recent target issues/PRs with no ClawSweeper review record and dispatches them; or
- split noisy activity notification from dispatch-critical review intake.

The fix should make cancellation safe: cancelled activity runs should either be superseded by a run that covers the same item, or leave durable state that a later backfill can process.
target.author: brokemac79
